exerciseDescriptions.squat: Keep your back straight and go down to a comfortable depth.
exerciseDescriptions.benchPress: Lower the bar with control and press without jerking.
exerciseDescriptions.deadlift: Keep a neutral spine and drive through your legs and core.
exerciseDescriptions.overheadPress: Press straight overhead and avoid over-arching your lower back.
exerciseDescriptions.row: Pull the weight toward your waist and squeeze your shoulder blades.
exerciseDescriptions.burpees: Keep a solid pace but maintain proper form.
exerciseDescriptions.jumpRope: Stay light on your toes and keep a steady rhythm.
exerciseDescriptions.plank: Keep your body straight and avoid dropping your hips.
exerciseDescriptions.mountainClimber: Drive your knees toward your chest quickly and with control.
exerciseDescriptions.highKnees: Lift your knees high and keep an active pace.
exerciseDescriptions.pushUps: Keep your body straight and lower yourself with control.
exerciseDescriptions.bodyweightSquats: Track your knees and squat to a comfortable depth.
exerciseDescriptions.generalPlank: Tighten your core and glutes, keep the body aligned.
exerciseDescriptions.lunges: Step confidently and keep your balance.
exerciseDescriptions.jumpingJacks: Move rhythmically and keep your shoulders relaxed.

armCircles.desc: Warm up your shoulders and arms.

knowledgePage.title: Energy, habits and recovery
knowledgePage.subtitle: This section helps the user understand their current state, get practical advice for today, and choose useful habits without overload.

energyCheck.title: Energy Check
energyCheck.subtitle: Answer a few questions and the system will show what type of fatigue you most likely have right now.
energyCheck.sleepHours: How many hours did you sleep?
energyCheck.mentalLoad: How overloaded does your mind feel?
energyCheck.screenFatigue: How tired are you from screens?
energyCheck.bodyPain: Do you have muscle pain / soreness?
energyCheck.bodyHeavy: How heavy does your body feel?

results.title: Assessment result
results.mentalText: Your mind is likely overloaded: stress, screen fatigue and lower concentration.
results.physicalText: Your body seems more tired than your mind. Recovery and lighter activity may help.
results.mixedText: There are signs of both mental and physical overload. Recovery should be the focus today.
results.balancedText: There are no strong signs of overload. A normal balanced day should be fine.

recovery.title: What is best to do now
recovery.mentalMain: Reduce mental load and take a short break away from screens.
recovery.physicalMain: Let your body recover and avoid training through strong fatigue.
recovery.mixedMain: It is better to reduce both mental and physical load today.
recovery.balancedMain: Your condition is close to normal — keep a calm and stable routine.
mentalActions: Take a 10–15 minute walk without your phone
Do 1 simple task instead of overloading yourself
Do not demand maximum output from yourself today
physicalActions: Add water and a proper meal
Do not heavily load already tired muscles
mixedActions: Reduce both mental and physical load
Do not force a hard workout
Choose earlier sleep or a short rest
balancedActions: Light or moderate activity is suitable
Keep your water and sleep routine stable
Do not overload the day with unnecessary tasks

habits.title: Habit Builder
habits.subtitle: Choose a goal and the system will suggest useful micro-habits.
habits.energyDesc: Small actions that help you feel more energetic during the day.
habits.sleepDesc: Habits for better sleep quality and recovery.
habits.recoveryDesc: Focus on recovery instead of overload.
habits.disciplineDesc: Micro-habits that are easier to keep every day.
habits.focusDesc: Useful when it is hard to concentrate and your mind feels overloaded.

energy: Drink a glass of water right after waking up
Get 10 minutes of walking or morning light
Avoid your phone for the first 15 minutes after sleep
Go to bed at about the same time each day
sleep: Avoid bright screens 30–60 minutes before sleep
Do not drink coffee in the evening
Air out the room before bed
Go to bed and wake up on a stable schedule
Avoid heavy workouts late in the evening
recovery: Take a 5-minute break after long mental work
Drink water throughout the day, not only in the evening
Do a short stretch after training
Do not try to do everything at maximum every day
discipline: Start the day with one simple useful action
Plan only 3 main tasks for the day
Do your workout even in a short 10–15 minute version
Prepare clothes, food or plan in advance
focus: Work in short blocks of 25–50 minutes
Take a small break after each block
Start with the simplest task first
If overloaded, go offline for 10 minutes without a screen

cards.title: Short useful knowledge
cards.sleepText: Lack of sleep reduces concentration, worsens muscle recovery and makes workouts less effective.
cards.waterText: Even mild dehydration can increase sluggishness, headaches and the feeling of fatigue.
cards.recoveryText: Rest is as important as load. Without recovery, motivation, shape and progress go down.
cards.foodText: Too few meals, too little protein or too little water can increase weakness and reduce performance.
cards.mentalText: If thinking feels hard, tasks irritate you and concentration is low, the issue may be overload rather than laziness.
cards.movementText: Sometimes a light walk, stretch or short warm-up restores energy better than sitting for another hour.

habits.reminderScheduled: A reminder will be sent to your email at {{time}}

home.mainText: This section is not only for reading. It helps the user understand habits, energy, focus, and core health knowledge that directly affect quality of life.
home.habitsDesc: How to build useful habits and change your life through small steps.
home.energyDesc: How to understand your condition, notice different types of fatigue, and recover on time.
home.focusDesc: How to reduce distractions, improve concentration, and stay on what matters.
home.healthDesc: Why exercise, nutrition, sleep, and recovery are some of the most important foundations of life.

videos.title: Recommended videos
videos.subtitle: For now these are basic recommendations. Later this block will show videos selected by our AI according to the user's request.

healthFeed.title: Useful knowledge without boring lectures
healthFeed.subtitle: Short, clear and visually pleasant lessons about sleep, energy, nutrition, recovery and daily state.

daily.label: Knowledge of the day
daily.title: Low sleep is not just tiredness
daily.text: When a person regularly lacks sleep, energy, focus, self-control and stress resistance all drop. That makes routine, food and productivity harder to manage.

lessons.title: Why sleep changes the whole day
lessons.text: Sleep affects not only rest, but also mood, appetite, focus and energy.

lessons.title: Why water affects how you feel
lessons.text: Even simple lack of water can increase sluggishness and the feeling of being low-energy.

lessons.title: Bad focus is not always laziness
lessons.text: Often the reason is mental fatigue, screens, overload and poor recovery.

lessons.title: Food affects more than body weight
lessons.text: Food affects energy, mood, mental clarity and the ability to keep your day stable.

lessons.title: Rest is part of progress
lessons.text: Without recovery even good workouts and healthy eating work less effectively.

lessons.title: No energy does not always mean weak character
lessons.text: Sometimes a person needs sleep, water, food, walking and lower overload — not more pressure.

lessons.title: Small habits are stronger than motivation
lessons.text: Motivation is unstable, but useful habits slowly change your lifestyle.

lessons.title: Why chaos breaks routine
lessons.text: When the day feels unmanaged, energy, discipline and concentration all drop faster.

myths.myth: If you have no energy, you are just lazy.
myths.fact: Often the reason is lack of sleep, overload, mental fatigue, lack of water or no routine.

myths.myth: More workouts without rest always mean better results.
myths.fact: Without recovery both body and mind overload faster, and progress gets worse.
Coffee may help temporarily, but sleep, water, food and recovery have a deeper effect.

myths.myth: To become disciplined, you need to change everything at once.
myths.fact: Strong change usually comes from one stable habit, not overload.

scenarios.title: What to do if you have no energy
scenarios.problem: If you feel completely drained, do not demand maximum from yourself first. Stabilize the basics.
actions: Drink water
Take a calm 5–10 minute walk
Eat something proper, not random snacks
Step away from screens for a few minutes

scenarios.title: What to do if you cannot focus
scenarios.problem: When your attention is scattered, it is important not to start 10 tasks at once.
actions: Close extra tabs and notifications
Leave only 1 main task
Take a short break without the phone
Return in a small work block

scenarios.title: What to do if you crave sweets
scenarios.problem: Sugar cravings are often stronger when you are underslept, chaotic, skipping meals or exhausted.
actions: Eat a proper meal first
Check whether you are actually hungry
Drink water
Do not shame yourself — look at the cause, not only the symptom

scenarios.title: What to do if motivation is gone
scenarios.short: Come back through one small step
scenarios.problem: When motivation is low, do not build a huge plan. Return through one useful action.
actions: Do 1 simple useful action
Do not try to restart your whole life today
Lower the bar for today
Return to rhythm through repetition

collections.title: 3 foods for energy
collections.summary: A simple set of foods and basics that often help keep your daily state more stable.
items: Fruit or a light protein snack

collections.title: 5 signs of overload
collections.summary: If you notice several of these together, you probably need recovery, not more pressure.
items: No energy even for simple tasks

collections.title: 4 habits for a better morning
collections.summary: You do not need to change everything. One or two simple habits can make the morning calmer.
items: Do not grab the phone right away
Start the day with less rush

collections.title: 3 things that block recovery
collections.summary: Sometimes a person formally rests but does not actually recover. Usually basic things are the reason.

habits.title: Healthy habits
habits.subtitle: Big change usually starts not with a huge push, but with small repeated actions.
habits.ideaText: The point of habits is not doing something perfectly once. The real goal is doing one useful action consistently. That is how a strong lifestyle is built.
habits.whyText: If a person wants to look better, feel better, and become more disciplined, motivation alone is not enough. A stable habit system is needed.
steps: Start with one habit, not ten at once
Make the habit very simple at the beginning
Attach the new habit to an action you already do
Do not expect a perfect result every day
Focus on consistency, not one-time maximum effort
tips: Do not build a habit through overload and guilt
It is better to do a little, but regularly
Try to track completed habits every day
If you miss a day, do not quit — just return the next day

ui.heroText: Here the user chooses a habit or challenge, understands why it matters, and enables reminders for daily life.
ui.chainPhone1: Did not take the phone in the morning
ui.previewWater: Hi! Don’t forget to drink water today.
ui.previewSleep: Hi! Reminder for your habit: try to go to bed earlier today.
ui.previewPhone: Hi! Don’t forget: a calm morning without the phone helps your focus.
ui.previewWalk: Hi! A short walk today will help both your body and mind.
ui.savedHabit: Habit settings saved. Later this will be connected to real email reminders.
ui.challengePreviewWalk: Hi! Reminder about your challenge: a 15-minute walk today can refresh your mind and reduce fatigue.
ui.challengePreviewWater: Hi! Don’t forget your challenge: drink water today and keep the rhythm.
ui.challengePreviewSleep: Hi! Reminder about your challenge: try to go to bed on time today.
ui.challengePreviewPhone: Hi! Reminder about your challenge: try to start the morning without your phone.
ui.savedChallenge: Challenge settings saved. Later this will be connected to real email reminders.

energy.title: Energy and condition
energy.subtitle: The problem is not always laziness. Sometimes a person simply lacks recovery, sleep, water, movement, or calm.
energy.typesText: Fatigue can be physical, mental, or mixed. When you understand that difference, advice becomes much more accurate: sometimes you need sleep, sometimes a walk, and sometimes lower load.
energy.mainAdviceText: First understand your condition, then choose the action. The best answer is not always to force yourself to do more.
signs: Sleepiness and lack of freshness in the morning
Hard to think and keep attention
No desire to work or train
actions: Take a short rest or go to sleep earlier
Go for a calm walk without overload
Drink water and eat properly
Reduce today's load if the body is overworked
Step away from screens if your mind feels tired

ui.heroText: This interface helps identify what kind of fatigue you have right now: mental, physical, or mixed. For now it uses smart local logic, and later it will work with our own AI model.
ui.physicalMiniText: Muscle soreness, body heaviness, lack of recovery
ui.mixedMiniText: When both body and mind feel tired at the same time
ui.surveyText: Answer a few questions. The interface will show your fatigue type and suggest what is best to do next.
ui.sleepLabel: How many hours did you sleep?
ui.headLoadLabel: How overloaded does your mind feel?
ui.screenLabel: How tired are you from screens?
ui.muscleLabel: Do you have muscle soreness / pain?
ui.bodyLabel: How heavy does your body feel?
ui.whatNowTitle: What is best to do now
ui.futureAiText: Later this interface will work through our own model: it will determine the fatigue type, confidence percentage, overload level, and give more accurate personal recommendations.

result.mentalDesc: Your mind is likely more overloaded right now: stress, information noise, screen fatigue, and reduced concentration.
result.mentalAction2: Take a 10–15 minute walk without your phone
result.mentalAction3: Do only 1 simple task instead of overloading yourself
result.mentalAction4: Drink water and switch activities a little
result.mentalAction5: Do not expect maximum output from yourself today
result.physicalDesc: It seems your body is more tired than your mind. Your body probably needs recovery, not extra load.
result.physicalAction2: Do light stretching or calm walking
result.physicalAction4: Do not heavily load already tired muscles
result.mixedDesc: There are signs of both mental and physical overload. Today it is better to reduce pressure and focus on recovery.
result.mixedAction1: Reduce both mental and physical load
result.mixedAction4: Do not force a workout through exhaustion
result.mixedAction5: Choose earlier sleep or a short rest
result.balancedDesc: There are no strong signs of overload right now. You can keep a calm routine and avoid overdoing it.
result.balancedAction1: Light or moderate activity is suitable
result.balancedAction2: Keep your water and sleep routine stable
result.balancedAction3: Do not overload the day with unnecessary tasks
result.balancedAction5: Keep a stable rhythm without extremes

focus.title: Focus and concentration
focus.subtitle: Today it is harder for people to stay focused on one thing. Constant notifications and overload make the brain tired very quickly.
focus.problemText: Modern life overloads attention. People often jump between tasks, screens, messages, and responsibilities, which makes deep concentration much harder.
focus.ideaText: Focus is not a natural gift only. It is a skill that can be strengthened through simple rules and a calmer daily structure.
problems: The habit of doing many things at once
Lack of sleep and mental fatigue
methods: Work in short blocks with breaks
Turn off unnecessary notifications while working
Do not try to do everything at once
Let the brain rest without screens

ui.heroTitle: See how strong your focus is right now
ui.heroText: This interface helps estimate concentration, distraction level, and attention overload. For now it uses smart front-end logic, and later it will work with our own model.
ui.lowMiniText: Mental fatigue, mental noise, constant distractions
ui.mediumMiniText: There is concentration, but it is unstable and quickly lost
ui.highMiniText: A suitable state for study, work, and tasks that require attention
ui.surveyText: Answer a few questions. The interface will show your focus level, distraction risk, and what may help right now.
ui.sleepLabel: How many hours did you sleep?
ui.distractionLabel: How easily do you get distracted?
ui.screenLabel: How overloaded is your mind from screens?
ui.fatigueLabel: How tired does your mind feel?
ui.switchLabel: How often do you switch between tasks?
ui.calmnessLabel: How calm does your mind feel?
ui.highDesc: Your concentration is currently quite good. This is a suitable state for important work, study, and attention-heavy tasks.
ui.highBenefit1: It is easier to do important tasks
ui.mediumDesc: There is focus, but it is unstable. Fatigue, notifications, task switching, or mental overload may be affecting you.
ui.mediumBenefit1: Concentration can be improved through routine
ui.lowDesc: Your concentration is low right now. Most likely this is influenced by mental fatigue, screen overload, lack of sleep, or switching too often.
ui.lowBenefit2: Attention overload needs to be reduced
ui.distractionSub: Shows how easily attention drifts away
ui.overloadSub: Shows how tired the brain feels
ui.foodHigh4: Food that does not feel heavy before an important task
ui.resetLow1: Put your phone away for 10 minutes
ui.resetMedium1: Work in a short 25–40 minute block
ui.resetHigh1: Use this state for important work
ui.resetHigh2: Do not scatter attention on small things
ui.resetHigh4: Do not overload your brain with extra switching
ui.futureAiText: Later this interface will work through our own model: it will determine concentration level, distraction risk, mental overload, and give more personal suggestions about food, routine, and actions.
ui.exerciseHeroText: Here you will find short exercises that help train attention, reduce mental noise, and hold focus better.
ui.rescueTitle: I really cannot focus right now
ui.rescue1: Put your phone away for 10 minutes

health.title: Useful health knowledge
health.subtitle: Exercise, nutrition, sleep, energy, habits, and recovery are not separate topics. They are parts of one system.
health.mainText: The meaning of the project is not only workouts. It helps a person live better: support the body, mind, energy, discipline, and overall quality of life.
health.sportText: Physical activity helps the body become stronger, supports health, and positively affects mood.
health.foodText: Proper nutrition gives energy, supports recovery, and affects how a person feels every day.
health.sleepText: Without quality sleep it is harder to recover, concentrate, and maintain stable energy.
health.energyText: Human energy depends not on one factor, but on routine, sleep, food, activity, and overload level.
health.focusText: The ability to keep attention is an important part of study, work, self-development, and discipline.
health.recoveryText: Progress is built not only on effort, but also on the ability to recover at the right time.
